title: How to calculate a discounted price
task: Apply a percentage discount to a price — e.g. 25% off $80.

excel.note: A2=price, B2=discount as a percentage (format the cell as %).

explanation: A discount leaves you paying (1 − discount) of the price: 25% off means paying 75%, so 80 × 0.75 = 60. Entering 25% in a percent-formatted cell stores 0.25, which is why the formula uses it directly. To get the discount amount instead of the final price, use =A2*B2; to add a markup or tax, flip the sign: =A2*(1+B2).
